response.rationale: “Harmonious” doesn’t naturally map to any clear themed subset on the board. I selected a broad mix of concrete/setting/production words as the least-bad approximate guess set, but the clue-to-board fit is weak.

The clue 'teens 9' is not a coherent one-word semantic clue for these board words; best effort is a weak, broad guess toward roles/people and production-related terms, but the match is largely speculative.
“Carriage” doesn’t map cleanly to board categories; closest reasonable associations are various transport/vehicle-adjacent or related setting/material words, with machinery as a weak functional link.
“planting” directly relates to starting materials and contexts like seed, soil, flower, and garden.
“Caliber” doesn’t naturally map to these board words; I selected plausible related items (especially military-rank/competence and industrial/process terms) but the fit is weak.
“vain” doesn’t naturally map to any clear cluster on the board; selected a broad, most plausible set of semantically related/abstract work/role/product/process terms.

Clue “reminder” doesn’t naturally map to these specific board words; selected a mixed set of plausible-but-weak associations (e.g., mask/altar as ceremonial reminders, microscope/cinema as things that could prompt reminders) while avoiding any off-board inference.
Sewing strongly connects to needle and thread; cloth is directly involved, and button is a common sewn-on item.

Clue 'civilization 5' doesn’t map cleanly to board words. Selected the closest broader-concept fits: cultural/scene (cinema), some civilization-like structures or items (altar), and generic human-world objects (mask, crib) plus a tool associated with progress/knowledge (microscope).
Clue 'colony 7' is under-specified; no clear colony-related word appears on the board. Selected closest science/bio terms that could plausibly relate to colonies.
The clue “profession” strongly matches doctor, teacher, lawyer, and soldier; the rest are non-professions.
Clue “disarmament” doesn’t clearly and naturally map to any of the provided board words; chosen words are the closest-ish but largely forced, so this is effectively noise.

“Darling” is a term of endearment and doesn’t naturally map onto specific categories on this board; the closest matches are people/roles (maid, educator, listener, specialist, scholar, builder, colonel), but the fit is weak and repetitive.

The clue 'conspiracy' doesn’t map cleanly to specific board words; I picked closest associations related to authority, secrecy, and coordinated actions (prison/colonel) and roles that could fit coordination or information flow (specialist/listener/educator).
The clue directly indicates animals; horse, eagle, and shark are all animals on the board.
“Rancher” most directly evokes farming/rural words; from the board, only barn and tent fit rural settings, while mud/wood/horizon are weaker but closest available outdoors imagery.

“Fraternity” suggests social/organizational or religious/ritual contexts, but the board lacks direct matches like “brotherhood.” Chosen closest available terms: “benefactor/solicitor” (members/organizers), “altar” (ritual), and “minor/outsider/darling” as weaker associative fits.
“Building” fits well with museum, prison, school, and castle.

“Dazzling” suggests standout/bright things; I picked items that plausibly relate to vividness/materials and high-contrast imagery, plus some scene-capable words (horizon, submarine) and technical/fiery effects (combustion).
Clue “following 9” is unclear/likely non-semantic; selected 9 plausible board items from a typical 'category' set rather than deriving from the phrase.
Clue 'vehicle' directly matches car, truck, bicycle, and train; other board items are non-vehicle objects/places.
“Carbine” is a type of firearm, but no gun-related words appear on the board. Selected words that are the closest remaining plausible targets, though the clue does not match the board.

Clue “blazing” fits best with a few high-salience action/heat-related words (hero, movie, switch) and loosely relates to museum/historian and other vivid/active board items, but the clue is fairly noisy for this set.

The clue "native 9" doesn’t map cleanly to a specific natural semantic category among the board words. Chose 9 plausible-but-weak fits (e.g., virgin/minor/outsider for "native" as in inherent/origin, altar/cinema/downpour as miscellaneous common associations) rather than a strong, coherent subset.
Trial most directly connects to court, judge, lawyer, and often results in prison.
The clue "water" directly matches the board’s water-related nouns: river, ocean, lake, and waterfall.
Clue 'dairy' doesn’t align well with any clear subset of 9 board words (no obvious milk/cheese/cow/cream terms). Chose the closest unrelated food/garden-ish and general nouns as best-effort.

Clue is unclear/inconsistent with available board words ("exercise" + number). Chose closest physical/activities and related terms (instrument/equipment/draw) plus other plausible action-adjacent nouns (auto/movie/museum/sea/garden).
All four are clear types of musical instruments and are the most likely selections for the clue “instrument”.
“pride” doesn’t strongly and naturally map to any clear subset of the given board words; guesses are weak, forced matches to likely ‘work/identity/status’ associations.
The clue 'informally 9' is unclear and not a natural semantic cue for any obvious subset. Chosen a mixed set of 9 plausible, loosely related everyday/role words from the board.
‘Sponsor’ can point loosely to people/roles (educator, scholar, specialist, listener, builder, colonel) and to supporting entities/actions (product, processing), but the match is weak and uncertain.

“External” most directly fits outsider. Other selections are weaker near-matches (virgin/minor as outside-of-role statuses; micelle as external shell concept; remaining picks are assorted external/adjacent senses).
Clue “sandy” doesn’t map clearly to any specific board category; selected the closest/most defensible visually or thematically related words, but the fit is weak.

Clue 'overweight' doesn’t directly match board words; best fit is likely food/packaging or confinement/ground contexts, so selected items that could plausibly relate to heaviness or being overweight. Some items are only weakly connected.
“Succeed” suggests achievement/production and people who create or develop results (product, processing, builder, specialist, educator, scholar).

“terms” is vague, so I picked a likely set of domain-like vocabulary/terms from different categories, prioritizing clear standalone words on the board (chemistry items and common objects/clothing, plus a few concrete nouns).
All four are common metals explicitly on the board.
“regret” does not naturally map to any clear semantic set among the board words; selections are essentially unsupported.

“Freeway” loosely suggests road/travel and nearby environment or infrastructure; best imperfect matches on the board are outdoor materials/locations (mud, stone, wood, barn, tent) and related industry terms (machinery, processing, product, wash).
A natural clue for 'car' would strongly point to core components like engine and wheel, the place it travels (road), and a close related vehicle (truck).
All four are common foods on the board: bread, cheese, banana, and soup.

“Informally” is ambiguous; best-fit guess is roles/people that might be used informally (maid, educator, scholar, builder, specialist, colonel) plus common informal places/contexts (tent, barn, prison).

The clue “library” doesn’t naturally map to these board words. Chosen closest tangentially: microscope/cinema as “places/tools for viewing/learning,” altar and crib as types of sites/objects associated with stories or institutions.
